isolation.tempDirectories: mktemp -d per test (cleaned up after each)

steps.action: Create temp dir with one existing entry 2025-01-15-alpha

steps.expected: Exit 0 with mkdir script on stdout

steps.action: Check stdout for date prefix + typed text
steps.observed: stdout contains 2026-04-13-quicktest with touch, echo, and cd

assertions.title: URL shorthand triggers clone from TUI

steps.action: Verify both produce date-prefixed dir name user-repo

assertions.title: Actions only apply to directory entries not create-new

steps.action: Type 'xyznotfound' to filter to only create-new entry, then press CTRL-R, then ESC
steps.expected: No rename dialog, clean exit 1, empty stdout
steps.observed: Exit 1, empty stdout, no rename dialog in stderr

steps.expected: No graduate dialog, clean exit 1, empty stdout
steps.observed: Exit 1, empty stdout, no graduate dialog in stderr

steps.expected: No delete marking, clean exit 1, empty stdout
steps.observed: Exit 1, empty stdout, no delete marking in stderr

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. Ctrl-T correctly generates mkdir+cd scripts with date-prefixed names, URL shorthands (both HTTPS and SSH) correctly trigger git clone workflow, and Ctrl-R/G/D are correctly guarded against the create-new virtual entry.
